title: Hard Lefschetz theorem and Hodge-Riemann relations for combinatorial geometries
speaker: June Huh
affiliation: Princeton University; Veblen Fellow, School of Mathematics
abstract: A conjecture of Read predicts that the coefficients of the chromatic polynomial of a graph form a log-concave sequence for any graph. A related conjecture of Welsh predicts that the number of linearly independent subsets of varying sizes form a log-concave sequence for any configuration of vectors in a vector space. In this talk, I will argue that two main results of Hodge theory, the Hard Lefschetz theorem and the Hodge-Riemann relations, continue to hold in a realm that goes beyond that of Kahler geometry.

proof_stages.label: Hodge-Lefschetz/Hodge-Riemann template
proof_stages.summary: The opening survey isolates a recurring package: a graded algebra or cohomology ring, a distinguished positive class, Hard Lefschetz isomorphisms, and Hodge-Riemann positivity.

proof_stages.label: Combinatorial log-concavity phenomena
proof_stages.summary: Chromatic polynomials of graphs and independent-set counts of vector configurations are presented as universal sequences conjectured to satisfy quadratic log-concavity, while deletion-contraction shows why direct induction is not natural.

proof_stages.label: Matroid framework and characteristic-polynomial conjecture
proof_stages.summary: Matroids are introduced via closure, flats, and independence, and the graph and vector-space conjectures are unified as the log-concavity conjecture for coefficients of the matroid characteristic polynomial.

proof_stages.label: Tropical linear spaces and Chow viewpoint
proof_stages.summary: An arbitrary matroid is encoded as a tropical linear-space fan, whose noncompact toric variety and Chow-equivalence behavior motivate expecting projective-variety-like cohomology even without realizability.

proof_stages.label: Matroid Chow ring and Hodge theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk gives the explicit generators-and-relations Chow ring of a matroid, its degree map, polarizations from strictly submodular functions, and states Hard Lefschetz and Hodge-Riemann for this ring.

proof_stages.label: Derivation of log-concavity
proof_stages.summary: The Hodge-Riemann relations imply the desired log-concavity inequalities, with the actual characteristic-polynomial coefficients obtained as limits from the strictly submodular ample cone.

proof_stages.label: Flip-connectivity proof strategy
proof_stages.summary: The proof connects tropical varieties of same-rank matroids by local flips that preserve the relevant Chow-theoretic Hodge package, reducing the theorem to trivial matroids where it is directly checked.

proof_stages.label: Convexity for intermediate tropical varieties
proof_stages.summary: To formulate Hodge theory along the flip path beyond matroids, the talk defines convexity for piecewise-linear functions on incomplete fans by local nonnegativity on links modulo global linear functions.
